content:
FRESHNESS: FIX: 16/40 Arm-B verbs coincide with v1’s subset; with the same human targets and a frozen predicate, this is not a clean fresh-item test of item-level generalization. The sentence strings are fresh, but the verb set is partly recycled, so the replication is not fully decorrelated from s248’s specific item composition.

PREDICATE: FIX: R1/R2 are reasonable, but R3 “95% CI overlaps v1’s 95% CI” is too weak and gameable. Overlap is not a replication criterion; wide/heterogeneous intervals can overlap even when effects differ materially, and a weak rep2 can piggyback on an imprecise v1. This lets a mediocre result read as success.

PROMOTION: FIX: A replication should not “license” promotion in any substantive sense unless the promotion rule is itself precommitted and non-posthoc. The contamination caveat helps, but “direction/correspondence not competence” still leaves room for rhetorical overclaiming if the outcome is memorizable lexical bias. Keep the scope narrow and make promotion separately adjudicated, not automatically unlocked by replication.

OVERALL: NO-GO
